schema_sync.policy: rules[].directory plus sources_dir.directory should mirror active page_types[].path_prefixes, excluding DB-only types with no path_prefixes. Run gbrain check-resolvable --strict after schema-pack changes.

description: Machine-readable brain filing rules for skill writes_to auditing. The active schema pack is canonical for page types; this sidecar mirrors its file-backed path prefixes so check-resolvable can detect skill/schema drift.

rules.description: A page whose primary subject is one person.
A page whose primary subject is one company or organization.
A meeting transcript or minutes. Propagate durable entities to companies/ and people/ pages.
A typed contract page whose primary subject is an agreement; link parties out to people/ and companies/.
Web-research output: what is new vs already-known about a topic, citation-checked claims, freshness deltas.
Report pages generated through the report/frontmatter contract. One-shot results should usually be delivered, not filed here.
Date-keyed summaries produced by the dream/maintenance cycle.
Health/training log pages whose primary subject is a workout entry.
Health/nutrition log pages whose primary subject is a food entry.
A reusable idea, framework, or mental model not tied to a single person/company/project.
Generative ideas the user might build, write, or expand later. Stub-shaped pages that mature over time.
A multi-session piece of work with owner/outcome/active work.
Lean working notes, planning satellites, and compact insights that are useful to retrieve but are not full concepts, projects, sources, or authored writing.
Prose authored by the user. Use instead of stale originals/ for authored thinking.
Personal-life content kept separate from work, excluding sensitive originals that should stay outside Brain.
Imported chat/thread artifacts where the conversation itself is the evidence layer. Cross-link durable entities and concepts from the page.

sources_dir.purpose: ONLY for raw data: bulk imports, API dumps, periodic captures, or raw external content that feeds multiple brain pages. A page with a clear primary subject goes to that subject's directory, not sources/.

notes: The ACTIVE schema pack is canonical for valid file-backed shelves; this sidecar mirrors it for skill writes_to auditing.
The PRIMARY SUBJECT of the content determines the directory, not the format or source skill.
Cross-link from related directories via back-links; do not duplicate content.
DB-only schema types with no path_prefixes, such as atom, do not appear as filing directories.

dream_synthesize_paths.description: Trusted-workspace allow-list for the dream-cycle synthesize/patterns phase. Keep aligned with active schema file-backed shelves; DB-only atom extraction is not a file target.
